# x-timeline-archive

## Summary

`x-timeline-archive` archives posts from a single public X/Twitter account timeline into CSV, JSONL, and raw JSON files. It can be used as a command-line tool or as a Codex skill.

Use it only for your own account or accounts you are authorized to access. Do not use it to bypass access controls, rate limits, suspensions, privacy settings, or platform restrictions.

## Important Files

- `README.md` - human-facing setup, usage, FAQ, and safety notes
- `SKILL.md` - agent-facing Codex skill instructions
- `scripts/archive_x_timeline.py` - main CLI
- `scripts/vendor/bird-search/` - bundled X search backend based on `@steipete/bird`
- `references/auth-and-network.md` - auth, cookie, and proxy troubleshooting
- `SECURITY.md` - responsible-use and secret-handling policy
- `agents/openai.yaml` - UI metadata for skill-aware agent environments

## Requirements

- Python 3.10+
- Node.js 22+
- `npm` when proxy mode installs `undici`
- `AUTH_TOKEN` and `CT0` cookies from an X session the user is authorized to use
- macOS is best tested. Other systems should use `--proxy off` or an explicit proxy URL.

## Typical CLI Usage

```bash
python3 scripts/archive_x_timeline.py \
  --handle example_user \
  --since 2025-01-01 \
  --until 2025-07-01 \
  --output-dir ~/Downloads/x-timeline-archive \
  --prefix example_user-2025-01-01-to-2025-06-30 \
  --max-rounds 140
```

`--until` is exclusive.

## Outputs

For `--prefix account-range`, the tool writes:

- `account-range.csv`
- `account-range.jsonl`
- `account-range-raw.json`
- `account-range-summary.json`
- `account-range-checkpoint.json`

Normalized fields are `id`, `created_at`, `username`, `url`, `text`, `reply_count`, `retweet_count`, `like_count`, and `view_count`.

## Agent Guidance

- Prefer editing `README.md`, `SKILL.md`, `SECURITY.md`, or `scripts/archive_x_timeline.py` for behavior/documentation changes.
- Do not commit `.env`, `AUTH_TOKEN`, `CT0`, exports, `node_modules`, generated proxy preload files, CSV, JSONL, or raw JSON outputs.
- Keep examples generic. Do not include personal filesystem paths, real private research targets, or session credentials.
- If adding network behavior, preserve conservative rate-limit and responsible-use language.
